What is Olympic

TheGreece in 776 BCthrough393 ADthe games of ancient Greece were staged. The Olympics returned after1503 years. The Olympic games of Greece took amodern formand thefirstmodern Olympics took place inAthens ,Greece, in the year1896.The founder or the man who generated the idea ofOlympicswas aFrenchmannamedbaron Pierre de Coubertin. It was presented in the year1894.

The termOlympichas derived from the word‘Olympia’.

THE GAMES

Zeusthe father of theGreekgods and goddesses was honored in the religious form of worship. The Olympic Games were a part of such a ritual. Arural sanctuary site, the games were held in Olympia in the western Pelopnnesos.

There was a sharing of the same religious belief and language spoken was also same thus the advent of Olympic shared the same sentiments and beliefs. Male citizens were the athletes. They all came from different parts ofGreecenamelyIberia, (Spain)in theWestandBlack SeafromTurkeyin theEast.

MountainOlymposis the highest mountain in mainlandGreece. The sanctuary was named after Mountain Olympos.Mount Olymposwas known for the home of the greatest Gods and goddesses

There were series ofBronze tripodsthat were found inOlympia. The sources fromDeutschesandArchaologischesInstitutesaid that these tripods were the prizes for of the early years of the early events at Olympia back in the9thcentury.

Modern Olympics introduced theracefromMarathon Northeast of Athensin the year1896.It was not an event of the ancient Olympic games. It was a race of40Km of distancefrom Marathon northeast of Athens to the Olympic Stadium.

The Torch was never a part of the Ancient Games. It was a part of the ancient games in the year 1928 in Olympic Games in Amsterdam. The modernOlympic Torch Relaywas introduced in the year1936OlympicGames in Berlin. TheOlympic oathwas introduced in1920.

Purpose of Olympic Games

Promotingpeaceand theOlympic principlesaround the world was the main motto ofOlympics games.TheInternational Olympic Committeewas founded in1984. The contribution to maintain harmony and develop humankind was the belief of the Organization. The movement and the Olympic principles had continued to carry forward its harmonious contribution to the society

The sports organization was based inLausanne, Switzerland. The International Olympic Committee was a non-governmental organization

The interlocked rings represented the colour'sblue,red,black,greenandyellowon a white background’s were thefive continentsunited by the Olympics. Each colour represented the colour's of every nation’s flag

Theofficial Olympic sportsarethirty -fiveand theSummer OlympicGameswas consisted oftwenty-eight sportsandthirty -eightdisciplines. Thewinter Olympicsconsisted of7 sportswith15disciplines.

Summer Olympicsto name a few wereArchery Badminton Basketball3x3Baseball Athletics,Boxingto name a few. The number of games played from the year1896 Athens -2020 Tokyo.The list of sporting events included in the Winter Olympics wereAlpine skiing Biathlon BobsleighCross country skiingCurling Figure skatingFreestyle skiing Icehockey

Paralympic Gameswere important inOlympicHistoryand originated in the year1948.It originated fromSir Ludwig Guttmannwho promoted the rehabilitation of injured soldiers afterWorld War 2

Important events in Olympic History

Women were allowed to compete in the Olympic Games.Helena de Pourtalesfrom Switzerland was the first woman to compete.

In the year1916the Olympic Games were cancelled due toWorld War 1.

In the year1936African American athleteJesse OwenswonFour medals. It was the year when the Nazi Germany fueled the opportunity to host against Aryan racial supremacy beliefs

In the year1980theCold Warboycotted theMoscow Olympic Gamesby the US

In the year1996 Olympic Gold medalist MuhammadAlilit the Olympic flame.

In the year2008 eight golf medalwere won by American swimmerMichael Phelps.

In the year2008eight golf medal were won by American swimmerMichael Phelps.

Few facts of Olympics

There werefemale athletesin the year2012Olympic Games. It was also known that Olympicgold medalswere all made of silver.

India in Olympics

Hundred and seventeen Indian Athletes will show case this year in Paris Olympics. Seventy males will participate and forty- seven females.

Rugby 7sfootball and ranking rounds in archery will start earlier. The opening ceremony will be held onJuly 26th.

Athletes from North-East India  qualified Tokyo Olympics 2020

The Cabinet approved Five sports disciplines -boxing,badminton,taekwondo,weightlifting, andArchery. It had approved under theMission Olympic.

The team that will travel to theFrench Capitalin theParis 2024 Olympicwill be a contingent of29 memberscomprising of11 womenand18 menfromAthletics.The team ofshootingwill be21andHockeywill be19.

Six peoplefromWrestling ,SixfromArchery,Boxingwill also havesixpeople.Fourpeople fromGolf,threepeople fromtennis,twopeople fromswimming,twofromsailing. one each forequestrian,judo,rowing, andweightlifting

IOC President Thomas Bach reminds the Olympic Values in the turmoil world

Today on23rd July 2024the President ofInternational Olympic Committeedelivered a very powerful message. Speaking on the occasion of the142ṇd IOC SessioninParissustaining theOlympic valuesofsolidarityequalityandhuman dignityfor all have never been more important at the time of deep troubling moments of the world. Such message ofpowerofOlympic valuesare delivered by the IOC presidentThomas Bachon the celebration of the greatOlympic Games Paris 2024.The message is delivered in the presence ofFrench President EmmanuelMacronat the venue ofLouis Vuitton Foundation. The venue is located in the very Park of thehistoric congressfoundation day in1894hosted byPierre de Coubertin

Flagbearer of the Refugee Olympic Team

The opening ceremony of theOlympic Games Paris 2024will be hoisted by  carryingOlympic Flagon behalf ofInternational Olympic Committee.  The two athletes of theRefugee Olympic Teamare boxerCindy NgambaandTaekwondo Athlete Yaha Al-Ghotanywill be the flagbearers ofOlympic Games Paris 2024.The announcement to such names of flagbearers was given during the142nd IOC Session in Paris.

37 Athletesliving in15 countriesand competing in12 sportsis what theRefugee Olympic Teamcomprised of as members There is a reflection of growing Refugees globally indicates the participation ofRefugee Olympic Teamis the largest in number forParis 2024. The increasedwarsandpersecutionandconflictwho flee their homes has taken the numbers into100 millionin the team representation ofParis.

The funding is done byOlympic Solidarityto the Athletes fromRefugee Athlete Scholarship.Olympic Refugee Foundationmanages the support Programme of the Team. It supports hundreds and thousands of young people in sport affected by displacement in communities.

The Olympian Artists Programmeis a programme of the creative talents of Olympians and Paralympians. The invitation is to create artistic produce and present in the programme led by theInternational Olympic Committee

On 25th July in ParissevenOlympian Artistwill display their oil paintings, photographs, papercuts, mixed media collages, sculptured quilt made of recycled fabric. The Olympian artists areLuc Abalo,ReneConcepcion,Grace Latz,Enzo Lefort,Brooklyn McDougallandCementine Stoney Maconachie

Martin Vidaurre: Inspiring Chilean athletes to aim higher

Chilean mountain biker Martin Vidaurre is gracious for the support of Olympic Solidarity. The effort to the boost has brought him to participate the major international races in the Paris 2024

It is more than winning a medal in the game but its an opportunity to present to his fellow compatriots and Chileans ability to perform in the sport.

To quote 'It is the biggest dream to have a medal in Olympics but there is an opportunity to proof and thus the room for improvement for the Chileans must be created and the capacity to compete must be shown'

Swapnil Kusale

Shooter Swapnil Kusale has clinched the bronze medal in the men's 50m rifle 3 positions final held at the National Shooting Center in Châteauroux on Thursday.

The 29-year-old from Kambalwadi village near Kolhapur in Maharashtra finished in third place with 195 in standing after 156.8 in the prone and 153.3 in the kneeling rounds.

By doing so, he has etched his name in history by becoming the first Indian shooter to win an Olympic medal in the men’s 50m rifle 3 positions event.

Serbian tennis star Novak Djokovic's bid for Olympic gold could come to an abrupt end as the uncertainty over his further participating in the ongoing Paris Olympics looms large due to a knee injury.

Djokovic said he was "hoping for the best" after aggravating his injured right knee, which had required surgery in June, during his 6-3, 7-6 (7/3) win over Stefanos Tsitsipas in the quarter-finals.

The 37-year-old needed on-court treatment and a painkiller after slipping on the baseline of Court Philippe Chatrier.

Manu Bhaker has stepped closer to an unprecedented third medal of the ongoing Paris Games after qualifying for the final of 25m Pistol women’s event.

Manu shot 294 in the precision stage of the qualifiers and followed that up with an emphatic 296 in the rapid stage. She thus finished with a total score of 590 out of a possible 600.

India pulled off an incredible upset against heavyweights Australia in their final Pool B match on August 2.

India stunned Australia in a thrilling 3-2 victory to win their final group game and enter the quarterfinals on a high.

By doing so, India also ended a 52-year wait for a win over Australia in the Olympics. This is also the first win for India against Australia since AstroTurf was introduced in Olympics.

Indian archers Ankita Bhakat and Dhiraj Bommadevara advanced to the quarterfinals of the mixed team event after defeating Indonesia's Diananda Choirunisa and Arif Pangestu in the round of 16 on Friday.

The Indian duo secured a 5-1 victory (37-36, 38-38, 38-37) at the Invalides. The fifth seeds hit five tens in their pre-quarterfinal win at Les Invalides.

Skeet Mixed Team (Qualification): Maheshwari Chauhan and Anant Jeet Singh Naruka -- 12.30 pm

Women's Team (Pre-quarterfinal): India vs Romania -- 1.30 pm

Women's Dinghy (Opening series): Race 9 -- 3.45 pm

Women's Dinghy (Opening series): Race 10 -- 4.53 pm

Men's 3,000m Steeplechase (Round 1): Avinash Sable (Heat 2) -- 10.50 pm

Men's Singles (Bronze medal playoff): Lakshya Sen vs Zii Jia Lee (Malaysia) -- 6.00 pm

The Indian men's hockey team led by skipper Harmanpreet Singh will take on world champions Germany in an exciting semi-final clash in Paris.

India and Germany have played 18 matches against each other so far with India leading the head to head 8-6. 4 matches have finished in draws.

India, who had clinched the bronze medal in Tokyo 2020 by beating the same opponent, will be looking to seal their berth in the final after 44 years.

The match will be played on August 6, Tuesday at 10:30 PM IST and can be viewed live on Sports 18 1 and Sports 18 2 channels and live streamed on JioCinema app and website.

Star shooter Manu Bhaker has been chosen as India's flag-bearer for the closing ceremony of the Paris Olympics 2024.

Manu expressed her gratitude and said she was honoured to be given the responsibility.

She scripted history by winning two Olympic medals in a single edition of the Olympics. The 22-year-old shooter from Haryana joined an elite list including PV Sindhu and Norman Pritchard to have secured two medals in the Olympics.

The closing ceremony of the Paris Olympics 2024 will be held on August 11, Sunday.

India's badminton sensation Lakshya Sen will battle it out against Lee Zii Jia of Malaysia for the bronze medal today in the men's singles event.

He will look to bounce back from his heartbreaking semi-final defeat at the hands of reigning Olympic Champion Viktor Axelsen yesterday despite a spirited fight.

All hopes will rest on his shoulders to bring home India's fourth medal and the first in Badminton in this edition.

India's full schedule on Day 11 of Paris Olympics:

Men's Team (Pre-quarterfinal): India (Harmeet Desai, Sharath Kamal and Manav Thakkar) vs China -- 1.30 pm

Men's Javelin Throw (Qualification): Kishore Jena -- 1.50 pm

Men's Javelin Throw (Qualification): Neeraj Chopra -- 3.20 pm

Women's 400m (Repechage): Kiran Pahal -- 2.50 pm

Vinesh Phogat (50kg) vs Japan's Yui Susaki in pre-quarters at 2.30 pm.

Men's semi-final: India vs Germany -- 10.30 pm.

India's golden boy and Tokyo Gold medalist Neeraj Chopra stormed into the finals of the men's javelin event after his first attempt, which also happened to be his season-best effort.

Neeraj's throw was clocked at an impressive 89.34m as the reigning Olympic champion spent just 10 minutes on the field at the Stade de France on Tuesday.

Indian wrestler Vinesh Phogat has made it to the quarter-finals of thewomen's 50kg category on Tuesday.

She caused a major upset by beating defending champion Yui Susaki of Japan 3-2 in the Round of 16.

Vinesh Phogat, who is riding high on confidence after beating defending champion Yui Susaki in the previous round, knocked out Oksana Livach of Ukraine to seal her berth in the semi-finals of the women's 50kg category on Tuesday.

The Indian wrestler won by a scoreline of 7-5 to get one step close at a podium finish.

Phogat will face Cuba's Yusneylys Guzman in the semi-finals today.

Vinesh Phogat has been disqualified from the Paris Olympics after she could not make the weight on the morning of her 50 kg gold medal bout.

As per sources, wrestler was roughly 100 grams over the permissible limits, which could lead to her disqualification. According to competition rules, Phogat will not be eligible even for a silver medal and the 50 kg will have just gold and bronze medal winners.

India's shooting sensation Manu Bhaker received a rousing welcome after landing in India on Wednesday.

She was welcomed by hundreds of supporters and her family, who braved a steady drizzle to give her a memorable reception in New Delhi.

The 22-year-old Bhaker won a bronze each in the women's 10m air pistol and 10m air pistol mixed team event -- where she had paired with Sarabjot Singh -- to script Olympic history for the country.

Wrestler Vinesh Phogat was hospitalised on August 7 in Paris just minutes after she was disqualified from the women's 50kg final at the Paris Olympics.

As per sources, the Olympian fainted due to dehydration. Phogat was disqualified as she was 150 grams overweight during the weigh-in on the second day of the competition.

Currently, Vinesh is in the polyclinic of the Olympic Village, where she is stable and resting.

Antim Panghal lost her opening bout against Zeynep Yetgil of Turkey in the round of 16 of the women's freestyle 53kg event.

Despite the defeat with a final score of 0-10, Panghal's campaign at Paris 2024 isn't over yet.

She still has a chance to compete in the repechage round if Yetgil advances to the final.

Indian women’s table tennis team lost to Germany 1-3 in the quarterfinal of the Paris 2024 Olympics on Wednesday.

Archana Kamath’s 3-1 win against Shan Xiaona was India’s saving grace in the match with the other three matches ending in losses.

Here is India's schedule of Day 13 of competitions at the ongoing Paris Olympics on Thursday, August 8

Women’s Individual Strokeplay Round 2: Aditi Ashok and Diksha Dagar — 12:30 pm

Women’s 100m hurdles Repechage Round (Heat 1): Jyothi Yarraji — 2:05 pm

Men’s javelin throw final:Neeraj Chopra– 11:55 pm

Men’s 57kg freestyle round of 16: Aman Sehrawat — 2:30 pm

Men’s 57 kg freestyle quarter-finals (If qualified): Aman Sehrawat — 4:20 pm

Men’s 57 kg freestyle semi-finals (If qualified): Aman Sehrawat —9:45 pm onwards

Women’s 57kg freestyle round of 16: Anshu Malik — 2:30 pm

Women’s 57kg freestyle quarter-finals (If qualified): Anshu Malik —4:20 pm

Women’s 57kg freestyle semi-finals (If qualified): Anshu Malik — 10:25 pm onwards

Men’s Bronze medal match:India vs Spain — 5:30 pm

The Indian Men's Hockey team will battle it out against Spain for the bronze medal at the Paris Olympics.

The 'Men in Blue' will look to secure India's fourth medal at the quadrennial event after the country's medal hopes suffered a series of setbacks of late.

The India vs Spain hockey bronze medal match will be played at the Yves-du-Manoir Stadium, Paris at 5:30 PM IST.

The match will be available for free live streaming on the Jio Cinema app and website. Indians can also watch the live telecast of the match at Sports18.

India's medal prospect Neeraj Chopra will look to defend his Olympic crown when he takes the track in the men's javelin throw final.

The Tokyo Gold medalist will look to push India's stagnant medal tally. His title defence will start at 11:55 pm today.

INDIA'S FULL SCHEDULE IN PARIS OLYMPICS: AUGUST 9

Golf – Women’s Individual Stroke Play Round 3 (Aditi Ashok, Diksha Dagar)

2:10 PM IST

Athletics – Women’s 4x400m Relay Round 1 Heat 2 (Jyothika Sri Dandi, Kiran Pahal, Poovamma Raju, Vithya Ramraj, Subha Venkatesan)

2:35 PM IST

Athletics – Men’s 4x400m Relay Round 1 Heat 2 (Amol Jacob, Rajesh Ramesh, Santhosh Kumar, Muhammed Ajmal, Muhammed Anas)

Wrestling – Men’s Freestyle 57kg Bronze Medal Match (Aman Sehrawat)

All eyes will be focused on Aman Sehrawat as the Indian wrestler will battle it out for the bronze medal in the men’s 57kg wrestling category.

Aman started-off well in this tournament by emerging victorious in his first two bouts owing to technical superiority but he was eventually knocked out by Japan's Rei Higuchi in the semi-finals.

Indian wrestler Reetika Hooda lost to top seed Aiperi Medet Kyzy of Kyrgyzstan in quarterfinals of Women's 76kg event at Paris Olympics.

This comes after Reetika defeated Hungary's Bernadett Nagy in the Round of 16. Reetika will now have to rely on the repechage round to win a medal.
